channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Trial of former President Sarkozy sheds light on France’s back-channel talks with Libya’s Gadhafi
item.description: The monthslong trial of former French President Nicolas Sarkozy over the alleged illegal financing of his 2007 presidential campaign is shedding light on France's back-channel talks with the government of then-Libyan leader Moammar Gadhafi.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suspected US strikes pummel Houthi-controlled areas of Yemen
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes pummeled sites across Yemen controlled by the Houthi rebels early Friday, including neighborhoods in the capital, Sanaa.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Lithuanian, Polish troops aid in 'search and recovery' for 4 missing US soldiers from Fort Stewart
item.description: A Lithuanian official told Fox News Digital about ongoing search efforts to recover four missing U.S. Army soldiers in coordination with Poland.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UK doesn't want to 'escalate' Trump trade war with auto tariff response
item.description: British Chancellor of the Exchequer Rachel Reeves warned against heightening tensions with President Trump during his self-declared trade war against nations that oppose his “America First” policy. “We're not at the moment in a position where we want to do anything to escalate these trade wars.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: A stabbing attack in Amsterdam wounds five people, including four foreigners
item.description: AMSTERDAM (AP) — A knife-wielding assailant in Amsterdam seriously wounded five people — including two from the United States, one from Belgium and one from Poland — in a stabbing attack Thursday on a busy shopping street, Dutch police said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Police use force to break up protests at a university in Turkey's capital
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — Police used pepper spray, plastic pellets and water cannon against protesters in Turkey’s capital early Thursday, the latest clash in the country’s biggest anti-government protests in over a decade.
item.dc:creator: ROBERT BADENDIECK and ANDREW WILKS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Secret documents on Nazis who fled to Argentina after WWII being declassified
item.description: Argentina will soon declassify all government-held files relating to Nazi fugitives who fled and settled in Argentina after the Holocaust, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Sudan opposition says peace deal has collapsed after arrest of its leader
item.description: JUBA, South Sudan (AP) — South Sudan’s main opposition party said Thursday a peace agreement that ended a five-year civil war has collapsed, following the arrest of its leader Riek Machar a day earlier.
item.dc:creator: DENG MACHOL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Danish TV reporter: Americans knocked on doors in Greenland's capital; couldn't find hosts
item.description: A Danish television reporter said American officials knocked on doors in Greenland’s capital, Nuuk, seeking local hosts for second lady Usha Vance's planned visit, but they largely got a cold shoulder.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: His brothers’ keeper: Liran Berman calls for urgent action to save his brothers, Gali and Ziv, from Hamas
item.description: Gali and Ziv Berman were taken hostage on Oct. 7, 2023, and their brother, Liran, has been fighting for their freedom ever since. He says his brothers were "the light of every room" they walked into.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel's parliament approves a key part of Netanyahu's divisive judicial overhaul
item.description: Israel’s parliament has passed a key part of Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s plan to overhaul the judiciary.
item.dc:creator: Julia Frankel, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Brazilian President Lula swipes at Trump as US imposes auto tariffs
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva swiped Thursday at U.S. President Donald Trump's decision to raise U.S. tariffs on a wide range of products, saying Trump should consider the potential harm to the American and global economies.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazil ex-President Bolsonaro will stand trial over an alleged coup plan. Here’s what happens next
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — A panel of Brazil Supreme Court justices unanimously ruled that former President Jair Bolsonaro and seven of his associates will stand trial on five counts, including attempting to stage a coup after the far-right leader lost the 2022 election.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Palestinians protest Hamas in a rare public show of dissent in Gaza
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Thousands of Palestinians marched between the wreckage of a heavily destroyed town in northern Gaza on Wednesday in the second day of anti-war protests, with many chanting against Hamas in a rare display of public anger against the militant group.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, FATMA KHALED and SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Brazilian ex-President Bolsonaro ordered to stand trial over alleged coup plan
item.description: Brazil’s Supreme Court accepted charges against ex-President Jair Bolsonaro over an alleged attempt to stay in office after his 2022 election defeat, ordering him to stand trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Search 'ongoing' for 4 American soldiers missing from training area in Lithuania: NATO
item.description: Four U.S. soldiers who went missing from a training ground in Lithuania during scheduled tactical training, NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: His brother’s keeper: Ilay David warns his brother, Hamas hostage Evyatar, is running out of time
item.description: Evyatar David's brother, Ilay, is fighting just to play music together again. For almost a year and a half, Evyatar has been held hostage in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Houthis claim responsibility for strikes against US ships: report
item.description: The Houthis in Yemen claimed responsibility for alleged recent attacks against the USS Harry S. Truman aircraft carrier and U.S. warships in the Red Sea.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Two military jets collide in midair stunt, crash to ground in fiery explosion
item.description: Two Alpha jets collided midair during a training exercise in France before crashing to the ground and exploding in a massive fireball, dramatic video shows.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia, Ukraine agree to Black Sea ceasefire following US talks
item.description: The Kremlin suggested it may not honor the terms of a Black Sea ceasefire with Ukraine until Russia is admitted back into the SWIFT international banking system.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US goes international for eggs amid bird flu outbreak
item.description: Imported eggs, typically used for pet food, are now being used in processed foods like cake mixes and salad dressings.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Prann, NewsNation

item.title: Oscar-winning Palestinian director attacked by Israeli settlers, detained by army
item.description: Co-director Basel Adra witnessed the detention and said around two dozen settlers — some masked, some carrying guns, some in Israeli uniform — attacked the village.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A Russian drone attack kills 7 in Ukraine ahead of ceasefire talks
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia launched a barrage of drones across Ukraine overnight Sunday that killed at least seven people, according to local Ukrainian officials and emergency services.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: America's European allies try to pry unspent money back from USAID
item.description: Three European allies provided millions of dollars that the United States was supposed to spend for low-income countries.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, JAMEY KEATEN, MIKE CORDER AND VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Oleg Gordievsky, Britain's most valuable Cold War spy inside the KGB, dies at 86
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Oleg Gordievsky, a Soviet KGB officer who helped change the course of the Cold War by covertly passing secrets to Britain, has died. He was 86. Gordievsky died March 4 in England, where he had lived since defecting in 1985.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, NewsNation
